result:
I’d make it so people reliably understood the consequences of their actions—emotionally, socially, and long-term.

A lot of harm doesn’t come from cartoon villainy; it comes from short-sightedness, bias, fear, or not really grasping how our choices ripple outward. If everyone had a clearer, more instinctive sense of “if I do this, here’s what it will actually do to others and to the future,” you’d see less cruelty, better policy, healthier relationships, and fewer self-destructive decisions.

It’s not as flashy as eliminating disease or poverty outright, but it’s upstream of a lot of those problems. Change the decision-making, and a lot of the outcomes follow.

prompt: If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
